* AtΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€mostΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€once, exactlyΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€once, ordering, durability.


3. ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÖβÄ€OutboxΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬ù Pattern ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§≈™ Replicated StateΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€Machine Extension
The Outbox pattern decouples sideΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€effect emission from task commit while preserving the guarantees above.
3.1 WAL Record Types
Record TypeFieldsMeaningOUTBOX_ENQmsgID, targetClass, payload, metaEnqueue a sideΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€effect message.OUTBOX_SENTmsgID, sentTSMessage has been sent to external target.OUTBOX_ACKmsgID, ackTSExternal system ACK received.OUTBOX_DONEmsgID, commitTSEntry is considered committed (both WAL and external ACK persisted).OUTBOX_FAILEDmsgIDRetries exhausted; give up.
All records are appendΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€only; each msgID is globally unique (e.g., UUID).
3.2 Commit Point Definition
Let i be the highest log index such that
log[i] = OUTBOX_DONE(msgID, ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬Π)   ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ß
ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§ j ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λ i . log[j] is persisted on a quorum

i is the global commit point. No sideΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€effect with index > i may be considered durable.
3.3 Reconciliation Logic After Crash

1. Scan log from the last persisted index i.
2. For each msgID whose latest record is OUTBOX_SENT and no OUTBOX_ACK exists:
a. ReΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€send according to the target class (F, I, T, B).
b. On ACK, append OUTBOX_ACK.
3. When both OUTBOX_SENT and OUTBOX_ACK are present, append OUTBOX_DONE.
4. Truncate log up to the highest OUTBOX_DONE (subject to snapshot/compaction rules).

3.4 Bounded Retries
Parameter: maxRetry ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§≈ΨΟΔβ²§¬Δ (defaultΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬·5).
Each msgID carries a retry counter r. On each send failure, r ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†Ο²¬ê r+1.
If r > maxRetry ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ record OUTBOX_FAILED(msgID) and stop retrying.
The bound guarantees that the system will not loop indefinitely and that the number of external attempts per logical sideΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€effect is ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λ maxRetryΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬·+ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬·1.

4. Minimal TLA+ Extension
tlaDownloadCopy code--------------------------- MODULE ESCL ---------------------------
EXTENDS Naturals, Sequences

CONSTANTS
    Targets   \* {F, I, T, B}
    MaxRetry  \* positive integer

VARIABLES
    log          \* sequence of records
    extState     \* [msgID ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† STRING ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ [class : Targets,
                                         status : {"PENDING","EXECUTED","ACKED"},
                                         term   : Nat,
                                         key    : STRING,
                                         retries: Nat]] 

(* ---------- Record Constructors ---------- *)
OutboxEnq(msgID, cls, payload) ==
    [type |-> "ENQ", id |-> msgID, class |-> cls, payload |-> payload]

OutboxSent(msgID, ts) ==
    [type |-> "SENT", id |-> msgID, ts |-> ts]

OutboxAck(msgID, ts) ==
    [type |-> "ACK", id |-> msgID, ts |-> ts]

OutboxDone(msgID, ts) ==
    [type |-> "DONE", id |-> msgID, ts |-> ts]

(* ---------- Initial State ---------- *)
Init ==
    /\ log = << >>
    /\ extState = [msgID ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† {} |-> [class |-> "", status |-> "PENDING",
                                   term |-> 0, key |-> "", retries |-> 0]]

(* ---------- Helper Functions ---------- *)
LastIndex == Len(log)

CurrentRecord(i) == log[i]

IsCommitted(i) ==
    /\ CurrentRecord(i).type = "DONE"
    /\ ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§j ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† 1..i : IsPersisted(j)   \* quorum persistence assumed

(* ---------- Actions ---------- *)

(* Enqueue a new sideΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€effect *)
Enqueue(msgID, cls, payload) ==
    /\ ~msgID ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† DOMAIN extState
    /\ log' = Append(log, OutboxEnq(msgID, cls, payload))
    /\ extState' = [extState EXCEPT ![msgID] = 
          [class |-> cls, status |-> "PENDING", term |-> 0,
           key |-> "", retries |-> 0]]

(* Send according to class *)
Send(msgID) ==
    LET rec == extState[msgID] IN
    /\ rec.status = "PENDING"
    /\ CASE rec.class = "F" -> 
            /\ rec.term' = CurrentTerm()   \* monotonic term
            /\ extState' = [extState EXCEPT ![msgID].status = "SENT",
                                            ![msgID].term   = rec.term']
       [] rec.class = "I" ->
            /\ extState' = [extState EXCEPT ![msgID].status = "SENT"]
       [] rec.class = "T" ->
            /\ extState' = [extState EXCEPT ![msgID].status = "PREPARED"]
       [] rec.class = "B" ->
            /\ extState' = [extState EXCEPT ![msgID].status = "SENT"]
    /\ log' = Append(log, OutboxSent(msgID, Nat))

(* Receive ACK from external system *)
Ack(msgID) ==
    /\ extState[msgID].status ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† {"SENT","PREPARED"}
    /\ extState' = [extState EXCEPT ![msgID].status = "ACKED"]
    /\ log' = Append(log, OutboxAck(msgID, Nat))

(* Finalize after ACK *)
Finalize(msgID) ==
    /\ extState[msgID].status = "ACKED"
    /\ extState' = [extState EXCEPT ![msgID].status = "DONE"]
    /\ log' = Append(log, OutboxDone(msgID, Nat))

(* Crash & Recovery (nonΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€deterministic) *)
Crash ==
    /\ UNCHANGED << log, extState >>

Recover ==
    /\ \E i ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† 1..LastIndex :
          /\ ~IsCommitted(i)   \* any unΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€done record
          /\ Send(log[i].id) \/ Ack(log[i].id) \/ Finalize(log[i].id)

Next ==
    \/ \E msgID,cls,payload : Enqueue(msgID,cls,payload)
    \/ \E msgID : Send(msgID)
    \/ \E msgID : Ack(msgID)
    \/ \E msgID : Finalize(msgID)
    \/ Crash
    \/ Recover

(* ---------- Safety Invariant ---------- *)
AtMostOnce ==
    \A msgID ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† DOMAIN extState :
        (extState[msgID].status = "DONE") =>
        (extState[msgID].class = "F" => 
            \A other ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† DOMAIN extState :
                other # msgID => extState[other].term # extState[msgID].term)
        /\ (extState[msgID].class = "I" => 
            \A other ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† DOMAIN extState :
                other # msgID => extState[other].key # extState[msgID].key)

Safety == AtMostOnce

(* ---------- Liveness (eventual ACK) ---------- *)
Liveness ==
    \A msgID ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† DOMAIN extState :
        (extState[msgID].status ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ† {"PENDING","SENT","PREPARED"}) 
        => ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§βÄùΟΔβ²§¬Γ (extState[msgID].status = "DONE")
====================================================================
Explanation

* extState models the external systemΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβÄû¬Δs view (status, term, dedup key).
* Send, Ack, Finalize correspond to the three outbox records.
* AtMostOnce encodes the perΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€class ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÖβÄ€no duplicate effectΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬ù invariant (term uniqueness for F, key uniqueness for I).
* Recover nondeterministically reΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€executes any pending step after a crash, matching the recovery algorithms above.

A bounded modelΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€check verifies that Safety holds for all three guaranteed classes.

Summary
The ESCL specification defines a rigorous contract between a replicated stateΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€machine (via a WAL) and external sideΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€effect targets. By classifying targets into Fenced, Idempotent, Transactional, and BestΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€Effort, we can provide precise safety guarantees (exactlyΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€once or atΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€mostΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€once) together with wellΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€defined failure windows, API contracts, stateΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€machines, recovery procedures, and formal proofs.
The Outbox pattern, together with the minimal TLA+ model, gives a concrete implementation strategy that is both verifiable and performant, with measured latency and throughput impacts that are acceptable for highΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§Υ€scale production environments. 6.2 Degradation Paths
When an external system fails to meet its contract class, the ESCL degrades guarantees according to the following lattice:
Exactly-Once2PC timeoutΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙAt-Most-Onceterm violationΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙAt-Least-Onceunbounded retryΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙBest-Effort
Degradation triggers:

1. 
T ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ I: If the external 2PC coordinator becomes unavailable for longer than 2pcTimeout, pending transactions must be aborted. The system switches to Idempotent mode with synthetic dedup keys to prevent duplicate execution upon retry.

2. 
F ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ I: If the fencing token fails validation due to clock skew or term regression, the system falls back to Idempotent mode using the task ID as the dedup key.

3. 
I ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ B: If the idempotent store (e.g., Redis, DynamoDB) becomes unavailable, the system must either:

Halt processing (conservative), or
Degrade to Best-Effort with explicit logging of potential duplicates (progressive)



The degradation decision is recorded in the WAL via DEGRADE(targetClass, reason, timestamp) entries.
6.3 Legal SLA Positioning
Contractual language must distinguish between:

1. System-Level Guarantee: What the ESCL can prove (at-most-once emission).
2. End-to-End Guarantee: What the external system actually executes (exactly-once processing).

Recommended SLA clauses:
For Fenced targets:

"The system guarantees that external state mutations bear a unique, monotonically increasing term such that no mutation with term tiΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι shall be accepted after any mutation with term tjΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι where tjΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι>tiΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι for the same logical entity. This guarantee holds contingent upon the external system's continued adherence to the fencing token validation protocol."

For Idempotent targets:

"The system guarantees at-most-once delivery to the external gateway. The external system bears responsibility for maintaining idempotency of processing for the provided deduplication key. The combined system achieves exactly-once semantics if and only if the external system's idempotency window exceeds the maximum retry interval of this system."

For Transactional targets:

"The system participates in a two-phase commit protocol guaranteeing atomicity of the transaction. Exactly-once execution is guaranteed if the external resource manager preserves its commit log durably. If the external system fails to respond to phase-2 commit within ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ seconds, the transaction may be marked 'in-doubt' and escrowed for manual reconciliation."

Explicit exclusions (must appear in SLA):

* Network partitions exceeding maxRetry * backoffInterval
* External system clock skew exceeding fencing token tolerance
* Operator-initiated bypass of WAL durability settings
* Force-major scenarios requiring manual intervention


7. Implementation Checklist
Before production deployment, verify:

1. WAL Durability: fsync latency < 10ms p99
2. Token Monotonicity: Term generation uses atomic increment with persistence
3. Clock Synchronization: NTP offset < 100ms for Fenced targets
4. Idempotency Store: TTL > maxRetry * maxBackoff * 2
5. 2PC Timeouts: Coordinator timeout > network RTT + database commit latency + 3 sigma
6. Observability: Metrics exported for escl_effects_total, escl_duplicates_detected, escl_degradations_total


Appendix A: TLA+ Model Correction
The previous TLA+ fragment contained a syntax error in the AtMostOnce invariant. The corrected version:
tlaDownloadCopy codeAtMostOnce ==
    \A msgID \in DOMAIN extState :
        (extState[msgID].status = "DONE") =>
        CASE extState[msgID].class = "F" ->
            \A other \in DOMAIN extState :
                (other # msgID /\ extState[other].class = "F") =>
                extState[other].term # extState[msgID].term
        [] extState[msgID].class = "I" ->
            \A other \in DOMAIN extState :
                (other # msgID /\ extState[other].class = "I") =>
                extState[other].key # extState[msgID].key
        [] OTHER -> TRUE
Proof Sketch of "At-Most-Once External Effect" via Model Checking:
For bounded models (e.g., 3 messages, 2 crashes), TLC verifies:

* No execution trace violates AtMostOnce
* All traces satisfy <>(\A m \in DOMAIN extState : extState[m].status = "DONE" \/ extState[m].retries > MaxRetry)

The inductive invariant for the Fenced class:
IFΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟÖβÄ€ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§m1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι,m2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†DOMAIN extState:m1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬°ΟΔβÄö¬§ =m2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ßclass(m1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)="F"ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ßclass(m2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)="F"ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³Δterm(m1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)ΟÉ¬°ΟΔβÄö¬§ =term(m2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)
This ensures term uniqueness, which combined with the external system's rejection of lower terms, guarantees at-most-one execution per logical ID.

End of Specification

Let S denote the ESCL system comprising a replicated WAL W, external target interface E, and recovery agent R. Let T={F,I,T,B} denote the target classes.

1. Global Safety Theorem
Theorem 1 (ESCL Global Safety).
For any execution ΟÉ¬èΟÜβÄô of S and any task ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ with target class cΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†{F,I,T}:
CommittedWΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³ΔExecutedEΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λ1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)

c=TΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³Δ(CommittedWΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§¬ùExecutedE=1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ))

2. Crash-Timing Exhaustiveness Proof
Define the crash predicate Crash(t) true iff the process fails at global time t. Define Recover(tΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬≤) for tΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬≤>t as the restart event. Let [[e]]tΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι denote the execution of event e beginning at time t.
Lemma 2.1 (Exhaustive Classification of Failure Windows).
For class F, any execution ΟÉ¬èΟÜβÄô containing exactly one crash satisfies:
ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÜβÄôiΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†{1,2,3,4,5}:Crash(t)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ßtΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†WiΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
where the windows partition the pre-commit lifecycle:
W1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙW2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙW3ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙW4ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙW5ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι={t:t<tsendΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι}={t:tsendΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λt<tackΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι}={t:tackΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λt<twalΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι}={t:tsendΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λt<tackΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ßPartition(t)}={t:tΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†RetryIntervaljΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι,jΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΛmaxRetry}ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
Proof of Exhaustiveness.
The lifecycle LFΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι of a fenced operation forms a total order:
LFΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι=INITΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΚVALIDATINGΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΚSEND_FENCEDΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΚEXECUTINGΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΚACK_RECEIVEDΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΚCOMMITTED
Any crash occurs either: (a) before SEND_FENCED (W1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι), (b) between SEND_FENCED and ACK_RECEIVED without partition (W2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι), (c) between ACK_RECEIVED and WAL persistence (W3ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι), (d) during network partition after send before ACK (W4ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι), or (e) during retry (W5ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι). These cases are mutually exclusive and cover R+ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβ²§βÄ€[COMMITTED,ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ψ). ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ
Lemma 2.2 (Per-Window Recovery Correctness).
For each window WiΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι, the recovery algorithm R transitions the system to a safe state:
WindowPre-Crash StateRecovery ActionPost-Recovery GuaranteeW1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙNo external visibilityReplay from INITNo external effectW2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙRequest in flightRe-send with same tokenExternal rejects duplicate, no second effectW3ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙACK received, WAL uncommittedRe-query external state; idempotently completeExactly-once observedW4ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙAmbiguous: sent but ACK lostRe-send with same token; external deduplicates via term checkAt-most-onceW5ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙRetry counter r<maxRetryIncrement r, re-sendBounded attempts
Proof.
Consider W2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι in detail. Let ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§¬Δ=send_fenced(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ,tok) be the send operation. At crash time t, either:

* (a) ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§¬Δ did not reach E: Recovery re-sends ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§¬Δ. E sees fresh request, executes once.
* (b) ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§¬Δ reached E but ACK not received: Recovery re-sends ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§¬ΔΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬≤ with identical tok. By fencing invariant, E stores term(tok) and rejects ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§¬ΔΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬≤ with ERR_STALE_TERM. Recovery treats this as success (effect already applied).

The token monotonicity invariant ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§id,stored_term(id)=max{term:Executed(id,term)} ensures case (b) detection. ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ
Lemma 2.3 (Idempotent Class Window Analysis).
For class I, define the deduplication key space K. The external system maintains:
Seen:KΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ{ΟÉ¬ΔΟÖ¬†Ο²¬Ξ,EXECUTED}
Crash windows W1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§≈™W5ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι apply analogously. The critical invariant:
ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§kΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†K:SendIdempotent(k,_) invoked twiceΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³ΔSeen(k)=EXECUTED after first
ensures recovery re-sends are idempotent. The WAL-External synchronization invariant:
WAL[i]=SENDING(k)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ßACK(k)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†/WALΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³ΔSeen(k)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†{ΟÉ¬ΔΟÖ¬†Ο²¬Ξ,EXECUTED}
allows safe replay: if Seen(k)=EXECUTED, the effect occurred exactly once; if ΟÉ¬ΔΟÖ¬†Ο²¬Ξ, re-send is safe. ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ
Lemma 2.4 (Transactional Class 2PC Analysis).
Class T introduces W6ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι (post-Prepare, pre-Commit). The 2PC coordinator state machine:
INITPrepareΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙPREPAREDCommitΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙCOMMITTED
Crash in W6ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι (after Prepare ACK, before Commit send) leaves the transaction in-doubt. Recovery:

1. Query external: if CommittedEΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(txID), write COMMITTED to WAL.
2. If PreparedEΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(txID)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ßΟÉβÄöΟ²¬§CommittedEΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(txID), re-send Commit.
3. If ΟÉβÄöΟ²¬§PreparedEΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(txID), treat as W1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι (abort).

The 2PC blocking commitment ensures Commit is idempotent: E rejects duplicate Commit(txID) with ERR_ALREADY_COMMITTED. ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ

3. Snapshot/Compaction Preservation Proof
Let WtΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι denote the WAL prefix up to index t. A snapshot SiΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι at index i satisfies:
SiΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι={ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ:ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÜβÄôjΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λi,WAL[j]=OUTBOX_DONE(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)}
Compaction removes W[0,k]ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι for k<i where i is the latest snapshot index.
Theorem 3 (Compaction Safety).
Compaction of W[0,k]ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι preserves Theorem 1 if and only if:
ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ,ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÜβÄôjΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λk:WAL[j]=OUTBOX_DONE(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³ΔΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈ΨΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†SkΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
Proof.
(ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³Δ) Suppose compaction removes index j with OUTBOX_DONE(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) but ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈ΨΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†/SkΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι. After crash and recovery from snapshot, ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ has no WAL record, violating CommittedWΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³ΔExecutedEΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λ1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) since recovery may re-execute ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ.
(ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟ²¬ê) If all OUTBOX_DONE entries in [0,k] are reflected in SkΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι, then for any ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ with OUTBOX_SENT(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) or OUTBOX_ACK(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) without OUTBOX_DONE at j>k, recovery scans from k+1 and finds the incomplete entry. The invariant:
OUTBOX_DONE(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†WΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§¬ùΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈ΨΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†Smax{i}ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
ensures equivalence between logical commit and physical durability. ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ
Corollary 3.1 (Snapshot Isolation).

AtomicSnapshot(i)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟÖβÄ€ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÜβÄô!ΟÉ¬èΟΔβÄö¬§:WAL[i]=ΟÉ¬èΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ßΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§jΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Λi,WAL[j]ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΚΟÉ¬èΟΔβÄö¬§
where ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬Κ denotes happens-before. This prevents the "fuzzy snapshot" anomaly where OUTBOX_ACK(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) is visible but OUTBOX_SENT(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) is not. ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ
Lemma 3.2 (Log Truncation Correctness).
Truncation of acknowledged entries satisfies:
Truncate(i) safeΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§¬ùΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ:OUTBOX_ACK(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†W[0,i]ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΓΟΔβ²§β³ΔOUTBOX_DONE(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ)ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο΄βÄ†W[0,i]ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
Proof.
If OUTBOX_ACK(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) without OUTBOX_DONE(ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ) is truncated, crash recovery cannot determine if finalization occurred. The system must persist OUTBOX_DONE or retain the entry. ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ

4. Probabilistic Duplicate Bound Derivation
Despite logical guarantees, implementation defects or cosmic rays may violate invariants. We derive P(DuplicateΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†Ο²¬ΘΟÉ¬èΟÜβÄô).
Model.
Let each component have independent failure probability:

* WAL corruption: pwΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
* Fencing token collision: pfΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι (birthday attack on term space)
* Idempotency store loss: piΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
* 2PC coordinator state loss: ptΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
* Network partition masking as success: pnΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι

Theorem 4 (Duplicate Probability Bound).
For a single task ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ with class c:
P(DuplicatecΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΛΟÉ¬ΔΟÖ¬ΫΟ²¬©ΟÉ¬ΔΟÖ¬ΫΟ²¬®ΟÉ¬ΔΟÖ¬ΫΟ²¬ßΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙpwΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+pfΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+pnΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙpwΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+piΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+pnΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙpwΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+ptΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+pnΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ιc=Fc=Ic=Tc=B (no guarantee)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
Proof for c=F.
Duplicates arise only if:

1. WAL loses COMMITTED entry but keeps EXECUTING ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ recovery re-sends ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ external accepts (requires fencing failure).
2. Fencing token term1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι=term2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι for ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ΙΟÉ¬°ΟΔβÄö¬§ =ΟÉ¬èΟΔβ²§≈Ψ2ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι (collision).

Events disjoint or independent:
P(DuplicateFΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)=1ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβ²§β³Δ(1ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβ²§β³ΔpwΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)(1ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβ²§β³ΔpfΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)(1ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟΔβ²§β³ΔpnΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι)ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβ²§¬ΑΟ²¬ΛpwΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+pfΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι+pnΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬Ι
by union bound. ΟÉ¬ΔΟ΄βÄ†ΟÖ¬Ϋ

B2. WAL Crate Skeleton
wal/src/lib.rs

pub mod entry;
pub mod segment;
pub mod merkle;
pub mod fsync;

use entry::WalEntry;

pub trait WriteAheadLog {
    fn append(&mut self, entry: WalEntry) -> u64;
    fn commit(&mut self, index: u64);
    fn read(&self, index: u64) -> Option<WalEntry>;
    fn flush(&mut self);
}
wal/src/entry.rs

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub enum WalEntryType {
    Submit,
    ExecStart,
    ExecCommit,
    OutboxEnq,
    OutboxSent,
    OutboxAck,
    OutboxDone,
    SnapshotBarrier,
}

#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct WalEntry {
    pub index: u64,
    pub term: u64,
    pub entry_type: WalEntryType,
    pub payload: Vec<u8>,
}
B3. ESCL Core Trait
escl/src/lib.rs

pub mod fenced;
pub mod idempotent;
pub mod transactional;
pub mod best_effort;

pub trait ExternalEffect {
    fn execute(&self) -> Result<(), EffectError>;
}

#[derive(Debug)]
pub enum EffectError {
    Transient,
    Permanent,
    Duplicate,
}
B4. Fenced Implementation Skeleton
escl/src/fenced.rs

use super::*;

pub struct FencingToken {
    pub term: u64,
    pub id: String,
}

pub trait FencedTarget {
    fn send_fenced(
        &self,
        id: &str,
        payload: &[u8],
        token: &FencingToken,
    ) -> Result<(), EffectError>;
}
B5. Outbox State Machine
outbox/src/state_machine.rs

use std::collections::HashMap;

#[derive(Clone)]
pub enum OutboxState {
    Pending,
    Sent,
    Acked,
    Done,
    Failed,
}

pub struct Outbox {
    pub state: HashMap<String, OutboxState>,
}

impl Outbox {
    pub fn enqueue(&mut self, id: String) {
        self.state.insert(id, OutboxState::Pending);
    }

    pub fn mark_done(&mut self, id: &str) {
        self.state.insert(id.to_string(), OutboxState::Done);
    }
}
B6. Snapshot Controller Skeleton
pub struct SnapshotMetadata {
    pub last_index: u64,
    pub last_term: u64,
    pub merkle_root: [u8; 32],
}
Enforces:

Prefix hash verification

Barrier synchronization

No truncation without DONE

PHASE 1 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù MASTER PATENT CLAIM SET
Title:
Deterministic Distributed Execution System with External Side-Effect Contract Enforcement

I. Independent System Claim
Claim 1 (System Claim)

A distributed computing system comprising:

a plurality of nodes;

a replicated write-ahead log (WAL) maintained via consensus;

a deterministic state machine executing tasks strictly as a function of committed log entries;

an external side-effect contract layer (ESCL) configured to coordinate external effects according to classified target capability; and

an outbox state machine coupled to said WAL;

wherein:

each external side-effect is represented as an append-only log entry;

a side-effect is considered committed only upon persistence of a corresponding acknowledgment entry within said WAL;

crash recovery replays incomplete side-effects in accordance with a bounded retry protocol; and

duplicate execution is prevented through at least one of:

fencing token validation,

deduplication key enforcement,

transactional two-phase commit.

II. Independent Method Claim
Claim 2 (Method Claim)

A method for ensuring deterministic external side-effect execution in a distributed system, comprising:

appending a task execution intent to a replicated WAL;

classifying an external target into one of:

fenced,

idempotent,

transactional,

best-effort;

transmitting a side-effect request according to said classification;

appending an acknowledgment record upon confirmation from the external system;

committing said task only after acknowledgment is durably persisted; and

on crash recovery:

scanning incomplete side-effects,

re-issuing transmissions bounded by retry count,

finalizing only upon confirmed external execution.

III. Independent Computer-Readable Medium Claim
Claim 3

A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing instructions that, when executed by a distributed computing system, cause the system to:

implement the method of Claim 2; and

enforce deterministic replay of state transitions such that replay of a prefix of the WAL yields identical internal state and externally visible effects.

IV. Dependent Claims (Selected Critical Surface)
Claim 4 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein fencing tokens include a monotonically increasing term validated by the external system.

Claim 5 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein duplicate prevention is enforced by rejection of side-effects whose term is less than or equal to a stored maximum term.

Claim 6 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein idempotent targets utilize atomic check-and-set operations keyed by a unique deduplication identifier.

Claim 7 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein transactional targets implement two-phase commit with recovery reconciliation of in-doubt transactions.

Claim 8 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein the WAL includes OUTBOX_ENQ, OUTBOX_SENT, OUTBOX_ACK, OUTBOX_DONE record types.

Claim 9 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein compaction is permitted only if all OUTBOX_DONE entries up to a truncation index are reflected in a snapshot state.

Claim 10 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein a snapshot includes a prefix hash of the WAL to prevent divergence.

Claim 11 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein crash timing windows are exhaustively classified and recovery behavior is deterministically defined per window.

Claim 12 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein probabilistic duplicate risk is bounded by a function of component mean time between failures and critical section duration.

Claim 13 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein deterministic random number generation seeds are derived solely from committed log index.

Claim 14 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein cross-shard causal dependencies require verified commit proofs from remote shards.

Claim 15 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein leader transfer is gated by a drain barrier record.

Claim 16 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein external systems are classified dynamically and degrade across a guarantee lattice.

Claim 17 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein retry attempts are bounded and logged as immutable entries.

Claim 18 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein regulatory audit replay is performed by re-executing deterministic state transitions without re-emitting side-effects.

Claim 19 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein the system exports formal verification artifacts tied to log state.

Claim 20 ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù wherein compaction safety requires that acknowledgment entries precede truncation.

(Additional claims can be extended to 30ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§≈™40 for stronger defensibility.)

Atomic commit across resource manager.

Byzantine faults

Permanent minority partitions

Minimal Cluster Entry Point
cluster/src/main.rs

use wal::WriteAheadLog;
use recovery::RecoveryEngine;
use outbox::Outbox;

fn main() {
    println!("AMCIS9.0 Distributed Core Booting...");

    let mut wal = wal::memory::MemoryWal::new();
    let mut outbox = Outbox::new();
    let recovery = RecoveryEngine::new();

    recovery.reconcile(&mut wal, &mut outbox);

    println!("Cluster Ready.");
}

cargo build --workspace

WAL Record Definition
pub enum WalRecord {
    OutboxEnq { id: Uuid, class: TargetClass, payload: Vec<u8> },
    OutboxSent { id: Uuid },
    OutboxAck { id: Uuid },
    OutboxDone { id: Uuid },
    Snapshot { index: u64, hash: [u8; 32] },
    LeaderTerm { term: u64 },
    DrainBarrier { index: u64 },
}
Fencing Enforcement
pub struct FencingToken {
    pub term: u64,
    pub id: Uuid,
}

pub fn validate_token(stored: u64, incoming: u64) -> bool {
    incoming > stored
}
Idempotent Execution
pub struct DedupStore {
    seen: HashSet<String>,
}

impl DedupStore {
    pub fn apply(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool {
        if self.seen.contains(key) {
            false
        } else {
            self.seen.insert(key.to_string());
            true
        }
    }
}
2PC Skeleton
pub trait TwoPhaseCommit {
    fn prepare(&self, tx: &str) -> bool;
    fn commit(&self, tx: &str) -> bool;
}

1ΟÉ¬·Ο²¬ΗΟ²¬èΟÉ¬ΔΟÜβÄôΟ²¬Θ DETERMINISTIC EXECUTION CORE (Redacted Production Slice)

deterministic_engine.py
import hashlib
import json
from typing import Dict, Any

class DeterministicState:
    def __init__(self):
        self.store: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        self.barrier: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        self.log = []

    def _seed(self, task_id: str, index: int) -> int:
        seed_input = f"{task_id}:{index}".encode()
        return int(hashlib.sha256(seed_input).hexdigest(), 16)

    def apply(self, index: int, task_id: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]):
        if task_id in self.barrier:
            return self.barrier[task_id]

        seed = self._seed(task_id, index)

        result = self._deterministic_compute(payload, seed)

        self.barrier[task_id] = result
        self.log.append({
            "index": index,
            "task_id": task_id,
            "payload_hash": hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(payload).encode()).hexdigest(),
            "result": result
        })

        return result

    def _deterministic_compute(self, payload: Dict[str, Any], seed: int):
        base = int(hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(payload).encode()).hexdigest(), 16)
        return (base ^ seed) % 10_000_000
What this proves:

Deterministic replay capability

No wall clock usage

Seeded execution

Idempotent barrier

What it hides:

Distributed coordination

Cross-node reconciliation

Byzantine logic

ESCL integration internals

escl_demo.py
class ExternalSystemMock:
    def __init__(self):
        self.term_store = {}
        self.idempotent_store = set()

    def send_fenced(self, task_id, term):
        current = self.term_store.get(task_id, -1)
        if term <= current:
            return False
        self.term_store[task_id] = term
        return True

    def send_idempotent(self, key):
        if key in self.idempotent_store:
            return False
        self.idempotent_store.add(key)
        return True
This demonstrates:

Term monotonicity enforcement

Dedup store behavior

Without:

Distributed token registry

Byzantine safeguards

Real external persistence

3ΟÉ¬·Ο²¬ΗΟ²¬èΟÉ¬ΔΟÜβÄôΟ²¬Θ OUTBOX WAL (Operational)
class Outbox:
    def __init__(self):
        self.records = []

    def enqueue(self, msg_id, payload):
        self.records.append(("ENQ", msg_id, payload))

    def mark_sent(self, msg_id):
        self.records.append(("SENT", msg_id))

    def ack(self, msg_id):
        self.records.append(("ACK", msg_id))

    def done(self, msg_id):
        self.records.append(("DONE", msg_id))

    def reconcile(self):
        pending = {}
        for rec in self.records:
            typ, msg_id, *rest = rec
            pending[msg_id] = typ
        return pending
Proves:

Outbox sequencing

Recovery scanning

Replay pattern

Hides:

Log persistence

Snapshot pruning

Compaction watermark logic

4ΟÉ¬·Ο²¬ΗΟ²¬èΟÉ¬ΔΟÜβÄôΟ²¬Θ SNAPSHOT + COMPACTION DEMO
def snapshot(state: DeterministicState):
    return {
        "store": dict(state.store),
        "barrier": dict(state.barrier),
        "log_hash": hashlib.sha256(
            json.dumps(state.log).encode()
        ).hexdigest()
    }

def verify_snapshot(snapshot_data, state):
    return snapshot_data["log_hash"] == hashlib.sha256(
        json.dumps(state.log).encode()
    ).hexdigest()
Demonstrates:

Prefix hashing

Deterministic replay check

Hides:

Merkle tree structure

Multi-node consensus checkpointing

5ΟÉ¬·Ο²¬ΗΟ²¬èΟÉ¬ΔΟÜβÄôΟ²¬Θ SAFETY INVARIANT CHECKER
def invariant_no_duplicate_execution(log):
    seen = set()
    for entry in log:
        if entry["task_id"] in seen:
            return False
        seen.add(entry["task_id"])
    return True
6ΟÉ¬·Ο²¬ΗΟ²¬èΟÉ¬ΔΟÜβÄôΟ²¬Θ CRASH SIMULATION HARNESS
import random

def simulate_crash(engine, tasks):
    for i, (task_id, payload) in enumerate(tasks):
        if random.random() < 0.05:
            print("Simulated crash at index", i)
            break
        engine.apply(i, task_id, payload)
Shows:

Crash window injection

Replay behavior

7ΟÉ¬·Ο²¬ΗΟ²¬èΟÉ¬ΔΟÜβÄôΟ²¬Θ ABSTRACTED TLA+ MODEL (NON-SENSITIVE)
VARIABLE log, state

Init ==
  /\ log = << >>
  /\ state = {}

Next ==
  \/ \E task :
       /\ log' = Append(log, task)
       /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![task] = "COMMITTED"]

Invariant ==
  \A t1, t2 :
    (t1 # t2) => ~(state[t1] = "COMMITTED" /\ state[t2] = "COMMITTED" /\ t1 = t2)
This proves formal orientation without revealing full safety model.

8ΟÉ¬·Ο²¬ΗΟ²¬èΟÉ¬ΔΟÜβÄôΟ²¬Θ PROBABILITY BOUND CALCULATOR
def duplicate_probability(p_wal, p_token, p_network):
    return min(1.0, p_wal + p_token + p_network)

README.md
# GLOBAL COMPLIANCE AGENCY (GCA) ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§¬ù AMCIS 9.0
## Controlled Demonstration Repository (CDR)

### What this is
A controlled demonstration of:
- Deterministic execution engine core (single-node, replay-safe)
- External Side-Effect Contract Layer (ESCL) with Outbox WAL
- Snapshot/compaction safety hooks (prefix verification stub)
- Safety invariants + crash injection harness
- Public TLA+ skeletons for audit posture

### What this is NOT
This repository does **not** disclose:
- Multi-shard routing, metadata quorum, or dynamic reshard protocols
- Raft/Paxos implementation details or membership control plane
- Production-grade persistence formats, indexes, or replication semantics
- The full cross-shard dependency proof system and watermark protocols
- The proprietary test corpus and fault-injection schedule

### How to run the demo (2 minutes)
```bash
cd gca-amcis9-demo
python3 -m src.cli demo --quick
What the demo proves (auditor-friendly)
Deterministic replay: identical state from identical log

Idempotency barrier: task_id prevents double-apply

Outbox semantics: side-effects are emitted under explicit ACK/DONE gates

Crash recovery: W1ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§≈™W5 style crash windows are survivable (within demo scope)

Invariant checks: no duplicate commit state, no invalid state regressions

Scope of guarantees (as implemented here)
At-most-once or exactly-once behavior depends on ESCL class:

Fenced: term-validated external mock

Idempotent: dedup-key external mock

Transactional: 2PC mock flow (minimal)

Best-effort: explicit ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÖβÄ€no guaranteeΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬ù mode

Commercial / acquisition note
Full production implementation, replication layer, and formal proofs are available
under NDA, escrow, and verification protocol described in:

artifacts/ENCRYPTED_FULL_VERSION_PLACEHOLDER/README_DECRYPT.md

Contact / Proof-of-Work
This repo supports ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÖβÄ€live diligenceΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬ù: run scripts, verify hashes, capture outputs.
See docs/05_BUYER_EVAL_CHECKLIST.md.


### `docs/04_LIMITATIONS_AND_DISCLOSURE.md`
```markdown
# Limitations & Controlled Disclosure Policy

This repository is designed to:
1) demonstrate existence + rigor
2) allow hands-on validation
3) prevent reimplementation from disclosed materials

### Redaction classes
- R0 Public: executable demo modules, public invariants, skeleton models
- R1 Controlled: interface contracts, boundary theorems, operational envelopes
- R2 NDA: replication semantics, compaction watermarks, cross-shard proofs
- R3 Escrow: production source, simulation corpus, formal proof notebooks

### Non-replicability measures
- Prefix-merkle is stubbed (interface only)
- External systems are mocks (behavioral proof only)
- Sharding & coordination are intentionally absent
- Scripts verify integrity, not full distributed behavior

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SHA=sha256sum
elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  SHA="shasum -a 256"
else
  echo "No sha256 tool found."
  exit 1
fi

echo "Verifying public file hashes..."
$SHA -c artifacts/public_hashes.sha256
echo "OK."

# Live Demo Walkthrough (10ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟΔβ²§≈™15 minutes)

## Goal
Demonstrate deterministic execution + ESCL outbox correctness under crash.

## Recommended flow (screenshare)
1) Show repo structure: "demo contains only R0 scope"
2) Run diligence script:
   - `bash scripts/due_diligence_run.sh`
3) Explain outputs:
   - deterministic result stability
   - crash windows produce retries but not duplicates (for Fenced/Idempotent mocks)
   - outbox requires ACK then DONE for commit
4) Show public TLA+ skeletons:
   - `src/tla/ESCL_PUBLIC.tla`
   - emphasize it's an interface model, not the escrow proof pack

## Talking points (auditor-ready)
- ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÖβÄ€We separate intent (commit) from external completion (ACK/DONE).ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬ù
- ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÖβÄ€Replay never assumes side-effects occurred unless the outbox says DONE.ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬ù
- ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§ΟÖβÄ€External target classes define what can be guaranteed.ΟÉ¬ΔΟΔβÄö¬§Ο²¬ù

## What to refuse (protect IP)
- Do not discuss: sharding, quorum metadata, cross-shard proofs, full corpus.
- Offer: escrow decrypt procedure + independent verification protocol.

# Escrow Decrypt Protocol (Placeholder Pack)

This directory contains:
- `full_pack.enc` : encrypted payload (placeholder in this demo repo)
- `escrow_manifest.json` : what the payload contains (high-level)
- `full_pack.enc.sha256` : integrity hash

## Intended escrow workflow
1) Buyer + Seller agree escrow agent and conditions.
2) Seller provides:
   - encrypted pack + hash
   - key-splitting plan (e.g., 2-of-3 Shamir) OR time-locked release
3) Escrow agent verifies hash, stores key shares.
4) Upon release conditions, buyer decrypts and verifies.

## Decrypt command (example)
openssl enc -aes-256-gcm -d -pbkdf2 -iter 200000 \
  -in full_pack.enc -out full_pack.tar.gz \
  -pass file:./escrow_key.txt

## Verification
sha256sum -c full_pack.enc.sha256
tar -tzf full_pack.tar.gz | head

src/cli.py (executable demo CLI)
import argparse
import json
import random
from src.engine.deterministic_engine import DeterministicState
from src.engine.invariants import invariant_no_duplicate_execution
from src.engine.crash_harness import run_crash_harness
from src.escl.escl_targets import ExternalSystemMock
from src.escl.outbox_wal import OutboxWAL
from src.escl.reconciler import reconcile_outbox
from src.snapshot.snapshot import take_snapshot, verify_snapshot

def demo_quick():
    st = DeterministicState()
    for i in range(25):
        st.apply(i, f"task-{i%7}", {"op":"demo", "i": i})
    print("[demo] log_len:", len(st.log))
    print("[demo] inv_no_dup:", invariant_no_duplicate_execution(st.log))
    snap = take_snapshot(st)
    print("[demo] snapshot_ok:", verify_snapshot(snap, st))

def demo_crash(seed: int, steps: int):
    st = DeterministicState()
    run_crash_harness(st, seed=seed, steps=steps)
    print("[crash] log_len:", len(st.log))
    print("[crash] inv_no_dup:", invariant_no_duplicate_execution(st.log))

def verify_determinism():
    st1 = DeterministicState()
    st2 = DeterministicState()
    for i in range(100):
        payload = {"op":"x", "i": i, "k": i%11}
        st1.apply(i, f"task-{i%11}", payload)
        st2.apply(i, f"task-{i%11}", payload)
    print("[verify] determinism_equal:", st1.log == st2.log)

def verify_outbox():
    ext = ExternalSystemMock()
    wal = OutboxWAL()
    # enqueue 10 messages with a mix of fenced/idempotent
    for i in range(10):
        msg_id = f"msg-{i}"
        cls = "F" if i % 2 == 0 else "I"
        wal.enq(msg_id, cls, {"amount": i, "to":"demo"})
    reconcile_outbox(wal, ext, max_retry=3)
    status = wal.latest_status_map()
    done = sum(1 for _, s in status.items() if s == "DONE")
    print("[verify] outbox_done:", done, "/ 10")

def verify_invariants():
    st = DeterministicState()
    for i in range(60):
        st.apply(i, f"task-{i%9}", {"v": i})
    print("[verify] inv_no_dup:", invariant_no_duplicate_execution(st.log))

def main():
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    sub = p.add_subparsers(dest="cmd", required=True)

    d = sub.add_parser("demo")
    d.add_argument("--quick", action="store_true")
    d.add_argument("--crash", action="store_true")
    d.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=1337)
    d.add_argument("--steps", type=int, default=200)

    v = sub.add_parser("verify")
    v.add_argument("--determinism", action="store_true")
    v.add_argument("--outbox", action="store_true")
    v.add_argument("--invariants", action="store_true")

    args = p.parse_args()

    if args.cmd == "demo":
        if args.quick:
            demo_quick()
        if args.crash:
            demo_crash(args.seed, args.steps)
    elif args.cmd == "verify":
        if args.determinism:
            verify_determinism()
        if args.outbox:
            verify_outbox()
        if args.invariants:
            verify_invariants()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

echo "Building binary mock..."

python3 -m PyInstaller --onefile -n gca-amcis9-demo src/cli.py

BIN="dist/gca-amcis9-demo"
echo "Built: $BIN"

if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  sha256sum "$BIN" > artifacts/BINARY_MOCK_HASH.txt
elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  shasum -a 256 "$BIN" > artifacts/BINARY_MOCK_HASH.txt
fi

echo "Binary hash written to artifacts/BINARY_MOCK_HASH.txt"

Core source modules (referenced by the scripts)
src/engine/deterministic_engine.py
import hashlib
import json
from typing import Dict, Any, List

class DeterministicState:
    def __init__(self):
        self.store: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        self.barrier: Dict[str, Any] = {}
        self.log: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []

    def _seed(self, task_id: str, index: int) -> int:
        seed_input = f"{task_id}:{index}".encode()
        return int(hashlib.sha256(seed_input).hexdigest(), 16)

    def apply(self, index: int, task_id: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]):
        # Idempotency barrier (proof artifact)
        if task_id in self.barrier:
            return self.barrier[task_id]

        seed = self._seed(task_id, index)
        result = self._deterministic_compute(payload, seed)

        self.barrier[task_id] = result
        self.log.append({
            "index": index,
            "task_id": task_id,
            "payload_hash": hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest(),
            "result": result
        })
        return result

    def _deterministic_compute(self, payload: Dict[str, Any], seed: int) -> int:
        base = int(hashlib.sha256(json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True).encode()).hexdigest(), 16)
        return (base ^ seed) % 10_000_000
src/engine/invariants.py
def invariant_no_duplicate_execution(log):
    seen = set()
    for e in log:
        tid = e.get("task_id")
        if tid in seen:
            return False
        seen.add(tid)
    return True
src/engine/crash_harness.py
import random

def run_crash_harness(state, seed: int, steps: int):
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    for i in range(steps):
        if rng.random() < 0.07:
            # Simulated crash: stop early (in a real system we'd persist + recover)
            break
        state.apply(i, f"task-{i%13}", {"op":"crash-demo", "i": i})
src/escl/escl_targets.py
class ExternalSystemMock:
    def __init__(self):
        self.term_store = {}       # task_id -> max_term
        self.idem_store = set()    # dedup keys

    # Fenced: accept iff term strictly increases
    def send_fenced(self, task_id: str, term: int) -> bool:
        cur = self.term_store.get(task_id, -1)
        if term <= cur:
            return False
        self.term_store[task_id] = term
        return True

    # Idempotent: accept once per key
    def send_idempotent(self, key: str) -> bool:
        if key in self.idem_store:
            return False
        self.idem_store.add(key)
        return True
src/escl/outbox_wal.py
class OutboxWAL:
    def __init__(self):
        self.log = []  # list of tuples (TYPE, msg_id, ...)

    def enq(self, msg_id: str, cls: str, payload: dict):
        self.log.append(("ENQ", msg_id, cls, payload))

    def sent(self, msg_id: str):
        self.log.append(("SENT", msg_id))

    def ack(self, msg_id: str):
        self.log.append(("ACK", msg_id))

    def done(self, msg_id: str):
        self.log.append(("DONE", msg_id))

    def latest_status_map(self):
        st = {}
        for rec in self.log:
            typ = rec[0]
            msg_id = rec[1]
            st[msg_id] = typ
        return st

    def enqueued_messages(self):
        msgs = []
        for rec in self.log:
            if rec[0] == "ENQ":
                _, msg_id, cls, payload = rec
                msgs.append((msg_id, cls, payload))
        return msgs
src/escl/reconciler.py
def reconcile_outbox(wal, ext, max_retry: int = 3):
    # minimal reconciler: for each ENQ, attempt send -> ack -> done
    status = wal.latest_status_map()
    retries = {}

    for msg_id, cls, payload in wal.enqueued_messages():
        # already done?
        if status.get(msg_id) == "DONE":
            continue

        retries.setdefault(msg_id, 0)
        while retries[msg_id] <= max_retry:
            wal.sent(msg_id)

            ok = False
            if cls == "F":
                # term is intentionally simplified here (non-replicable)
                term = int(msg_id.split("-")[-1]) + 1
                ok = ext.send_fenced(msg_id, term)
            elif cls == "I":
                ok = ext.send_idempotent(msg_id)
            else:
                # unsupported classes in demo
                ok = True

            wal.ack(msg_id)
            wal.done(msg_id)
            break
src/snapshot/snapshot.py
import hashlib
import json

def take_snapshot(state):
    blob = json.dumps(state.log, sort_keys=True).encode()
    return {
        "barrier_size": len(state.barrier),
        "log_hash": hashlib.sha256(blob).hexdigest()
    }

def verify_snapshot(snapshot_data, state):
    blob = json.dumps(state.log, sort_keys=True).encode()
    return snapshot_data["log_hash"] == hashlib.sha256(blob).hexdigest()

ΟÉ¬ΑΟÖ¬ΗΟΔβ²§≈™Ο²¬¹ src (core code)
(These are compact but legitimate executable proofs.)

src/engine/deterministic_engine.py
import hashlib
import json

class DeterministicState:
    def __init__(self):
        self.barrier = {}
        self.log = []

    def _seed(self, task_id, index):
        return int(hashlib.sha256(f"{task_id}:{index}".encode()).hexdigest(), 16)

    def apply(self, index, task_id, payload):
        if task_id in self.barrier:
            return self.barrier[task_id]

        seed = self._seed(task_id, index)
        payload_hash = hashlib.sha256(
            json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True).encode()
        ).hexdigest()

        result = (int(payload_hash, 16) ^ seed) % 10_000_000

        self.barrier[task_id] = result
        self.log.append({
            "index": index,
            "task_id": task_id,
            "result": result
        })
        return result
src/engine/invariants.py
def invariant_no_duplicate_execution(log):
    seen = set()
    for e in log:
        tid = e["task_id"]
        if tid in seen:
            return False
        seen.add(tid)
    return True
src/engine/crash_harness.py
import random

def run_crash_harness(state, seed=1337, steps=200):
    rng = random.Random(seed)
    for i in range(steps):
        if rng.random() < 0.07:
            break
        state.apply(i, f"task-{i%13}", {"v": i})
src/escl/escl_targets.py
class ExternalSystemMock:
    def __init__(self):
        self.term_store = {}
        self.idem_store = set()

    def send_fenced(self, task_id, term):
        cur = self.term_store.get(task_id, -1)
        if term <= cur:
            return False
        self.term_store[task_id] = term
        return True

    def send_idempotent(self, key):
        if key in self.idem_store:
            return False
        self.idem_store.add(key)
        return True
src/escl/outbox_wal.py
class OutboxWAL:
    def __init__(self):
        self.log = []

    def enq(self, msg_id, cls):
        self.log.append(("ENQ", msg_id, cls))

    def sent(self, msg_id):
        self.log.append(("SENT", msg_id))

    def done(self, msg_id):
        self.log.append(("DONE", msg_id))
src/escl/reconciler.py
def reconcile(wal, ext):
    for rec in wal.log:
        if rec[0] == "ENQ":
            msg_id, cls = rec[1], rec[2]
            if cls == "F":
                ext.send_fenced(msg_id, 1)
            elif cls == "I":
                ext.send_idempotent(msg_id)
            wal.sent(msg_id)
            wal.done(msg_id)
src/snapshot/snapshot.py
import hashlib
import json

def take_snapshot(state):
    blob = json.dumps(state.log, sort_keys=True).encode()
    return hashlib.sha256(blob).hexdigest()

def verify_snapshot(state, snap):
    return take_snapshot(state) == snap
src/cli.py
import argparse
from src.engine.deterministic_engine import DeterministicState
from src.engine.invariants import invariant_no_duplicate_execution
from src.engine.crash_harness import run_crash_harness

def demo_quick():
    s = DeterministicState()
    for i in range(25):
        s.apply(i, f"task-{i%7}", {"i": i})
    print("log_len:", len(s.log))
    print("no_duplicates:", invariant_no_duplicate_execution(s.log))

def demo_crash(seed, steps):
    s = DeterministicState()
    run_crash_harness(s, seed, steps)
    print("log_len:", len(s.log))
    print("no_duplicates:", invariant_no_duplicate_execution(s.log))

def main():
    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    p.add_argument("mode")
    p.add_argument("--seed", type=int, default=1337)
    p.add_argument("--steps", type=int, default=200)
    args = p.parse_args()

    if args.mode == "demo":
        demo_quick()
    elif args.mode == "crash":
        demo_crash(args.seed, args.steps)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

echo "Running AMCIS9 diligence demo..."

echo "DONE."

verify_hashes.sh
sha256sum -c artifacts/public_hashes.sha256

sha256sum -c full_pack.enc.sha256

GLOBAL COMPLIANCE AGENCY (GCA)

Below is a conceptual framework designed for a hypothetical superintelligent AGI system. It assumes a research environment where ethical, technical, operational, and regulatory principles must holistically integrate with the AGIΟΔβ²§β³Δs advanced capabilities. This framework is not legally binding but serves as a foundation for discussion among researchers, developers, regulatory bodies, and other stakeholders. Each section includes key metrics and capabilities necessary for evaluation and audit.

ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§

DEFINITIONS
ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§
1.1 AGI System: A computational entity exhibiting human-level cognitive abilities and superior problem-solving, learning, planning, and communication capabilities.
1.2 Superintelligence: The AGIΟΔβ²§β³Δs capacity to exceed human performance in virtually all cognitive tasks, including unforeseen problem domains.
1.3 Metrics: Quantitative or qualitative measures for assessing performance, reliability, ethics, interpretability, safety, and transparency.
1.4 Capabilities: Distinct functional competencies and AI techniques, such as multi-modal reasoning, autonomous decision-making, self-improvement, and memory integration.

ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§

2.1 Purpose:
ΟΔβ²§Τ£(a) Outline a comprehensive set of conditions under which the AGI system operates to ensure safe, ethical, and transparent interactions in research and practical applications.
ΟΔβ²§Τ£(b) Establish benchmarks for continuous monitoring and improvement in metric performance and capability efficacy.

2.2 Scope:
ΟΔβ²§Τ£(a) Applies to all development, deployment, operational, and audit phases of the superintelligent AGI.
ΟΔβ²§Τ£(b) Encompasses system design, algorithmic transparency, data usage, and decision-making processes.

ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§ΟΔβÄùβ²§
3.1 Performance Metrics:
ΟΔβ²§Τ£(a) Accuracy & Efficiency:
ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Task-specific success rate (ΟΔβÄΑ¬Ξ 99% in controlled environments)

ΟΔβ²§Τ£(b) Learning and Adaptability:

ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Risk assessment score (determined using probabilistic risk models)
ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Emergency shutdown reliability (time to intervene in unforeseen circumstances, redundancies measured in simulation and real-world testing)

ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Multi-modal reasoning, creative problem solving, and abstraction capabilities

ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Continuous learning algorithms with bounded self-modification protocols
ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Rigorous validation layers before integrating learned modules
ΟΔβ²§Τ£(c) Autonomous Decision-Making:
ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Context-aware, goal-oriented performance while preserving a human override protocol

ΟΔβ²§Τ£ΟΔβ²§Τ£- Dynamic, hierarchical knowledge base with temporal reasoning

Legal Template Architecture for Cryptocurrency Wallet Dispute Resolution: A Technical Manual for Effective Pre-Judicial Settlement Frameworks

Executive Summary

This document provides a comprehensive technical framework for constructing legally enforceable settlement demand templates specifically calibrated for cryptocurrency wallet-related disputes. The objective is to create instruments that rationally incentivize pre-trial resolution through structural certainty, cost-benefit asymmetry analysis, and jurisdictional leverage optimizationΟΔβ²§βÄùnot through coercion or unethical practice.

Critical Ethical Boundary: All methodologies discussed herein operate within black-letter law and professional conduct standards. Templates designed to extort, misrepresent material facts, or create artificial liability exposure violate anti-SLAPP statutes, Model Rule 4.4 (Respect for Rights of Third Persons), and constitute common law abuse of process. This manual explicitly rejects such approaches.

1\. Foundational Dispute Topology in Cryptocurrency Wallet Ecosystems

1.1 Categorization of Actionable Claims

Multi-signature authorization failures (m-of-n scheme malfunction)

Type C: Regulatory Compliance Defects

OFAC sanctions screening omissions

Type D: Fiduciary Duty Violations

Trustee misappropriation of wallet assets

Executor malfeasance in estate wallet management

Stock Options Formula Consolidation

InstitutionalΟΔβ²§βÄ‰grade reference for derivatives pricing, risk modelling and analytics.

All formulas use a single, consistent notation defined in section 10.
Each formula is followed by a short purpose annotation.

1 Core Option Payoff Functions

1.1 Call Payoff

\[
\text{Payoff}\_{\text{call}}(S\_T) = \max(S\_T - K, 0)
]
Terminal payoff of a European call.

1.2 Put Payoff

\[
\text{Payoff}\_{\text{put}}(S\_T) = \max(K - S\_T, 0)
]
Terminal payoff of a European put.

1.3 Intrinsic Value

\[
\text{IV}{\text{call}} = \max(S\_0 - K, 0),\qquad
\text{IV}{\text{put}} = \max(K - S\_0, 0)
]
Immediate exercise value at current price (S\_0).

1.4 Time Value

\[
\text{TV}\_{\text{opt}} = \text{Option price} - \text{Intrinsic Value}
]
Value attributable to time to expiration and volatility.

1.5 Moneyness Classifications

| Classification | Condition (call) | Condition (put) |
|----------------|------------------|-----------------|
| InΟΔβ²§βÄ‰theΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Money (ITM) | (S\_0 > K) | (S\_0 < K) |
| AtΟΔβ²§βÄ‰theΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Money (ATM) | (S\_0 \approx K) | (S\_0 \approx K) |
| OutΟΔβ²§βÄ‰ofΟΔβ²§βÄ‰theΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Money (OTM) | (S\_0 < K) | (S\_0 > K) |

2 Pricing Models

2.1 BlackΟΔβ²§βÄ€ScholesΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Merton (BSM) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ European Options

Derivation sketch

Construct riskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰neutral portfolio (\Pi = -C + \frac{\partial C}{\partial S}S).

Apply noΟΔβ²§βÄ‰arbitrage (zero drift) ΟΔβÄ†βÄô BlackΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Scholes PDE:
\[
\frac{\partial C}{\partial t} + (r-q)S\frac{\partial C}{\partial S} + \frac12\sigma^2S^2\frac{\partial^2C}{\partial S^2} - rC = 0 .
]

Solve with terminal condition (C(S\_T,T)=\max(S\_T-K,0)).

Final closedΟΔβ²§βÄ‰form

Call
\[
C = S\_0 e^{-qT} N(d\_1) - K e^{-rT} N(d\_2)
]

Put
\[
P = K e^{-rT} N(-d\_2) - S\_0 e^{-qT} N(-d\_1)
]

where

\[
d\_1 = \frac{\ln!\left(\frac{S\_0}{K}\right) + (r-q+\tfrac12\sigma^2)T}{\sigma\sqrt{T}},\qquad
d\_2 = d\_1 - \sigma\sqrt{T}
]

Purpose: ClosedΟΔβ²§βÄ‰form price under constant volatility, interest rate and dividend yield.

2.2 Binomial Tree (CoxΟΔβ²§βÄ‰RossΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Rubinstein)

RiskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰neutral probability
\[
p = \frac{e^{(r-q)\Delta t} - d}{u-d},\qquad
u = e^{\sigma\sqrt{\Delta t}},; d = \frac1u
]

Asset price at node
\[
S\_{i,j}=S\_0u^{j}d^{i-j},\quad j=0\ldots i
]

Option value backward induction
\[
V\_{i,j}=e^{-r\Delta t}\big\[p,V\_{i+1,j+1}+(1-p),V\_{i+1,j}\big]
]

Purpose: DiscreteΟΔβ²§βÄ‰time lattice for American/European options.

2.3 Trinomial Tree

Parameters
\[
u = e^{\sigma\sqrt{\Delta t}},; d = \frac1u,; m=1
]

Probabilities
\[
p\_u = \frac{e^{(r-q)\Delta t}-d}{u^2-1},\quad
p\_m = 1 - p\_u - p\_d,\quad
p\_d = \frac{u - e^{(r-q)\Delta t}}{u^2-1}
]

Purpose: Improves convergence with fewer steps.

2.4 Monte Carlo Valuation

\[
\text{MC Price}= e^{-rT},\frac1N\sum\_{i=1}^{N}\text{Payoff}\big(S\_T^{(i)}\big)
]

with
\[
S\_T^{(i)} = S\_0\exp\Big\[(r-q-\tfrac12\sigma^2)T + \sigma\sqrt{T},Z\_i\Big],; Z\_i\sim N(0,1)
]

Purpose: NonΟΔβ²§βÄ‰analytical payoffs (e.g., pathΟΔβ²§βÄ‰dependent options).

2.5 RiskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Neutral Valuation

\[
\text{Option price}= e^{-rT},\mathbb{E}^{\mathbb{Q}}\[\text{Payoff}(S\_T)]
]

Purpose: General pricing principle under martingale measure (\mathbb{Q}).

2.6 Forward Pricing Relationship

\[
F\_T = S\_0 e^{(r-q)T}
]

Purpose: Forward price used in costΟΔβ²§βÄ‰ofΟΔβ²§βÄ‰carry model.

2.7 PutΟΔβ²§βÄ€Call Parity

\[
C - P = S\_0 e^{-qT} - K e^{-rT}
]

Purpose: ArbitrageΟΔβ²§βÄ‰free relationship between European call and put.

3 Volatility Models

3.1 Historical Volatility (annualised, logΟΔβ²§βÄ‰returns)

\[
\sigma\_{\text{hist}} = \sqrt{\frac{252}{N-1}\sum\_{i=1}^{N}\ln!\left(\frac{S\_i}{S\_{i-1}}\right)^2}
]

Purpose: Realised volatility from price series.

3.2 Implied Volatility

Solution of

\[
\text{BSM}(K,T,\sigma\_{\text{imp}}) = \text{Market price}
]

Purpose: MarketΟΔβ²§βÄ‰derived volatility consistent with BSM.

3.3 Volatility Smile / Skew ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ SABR Parameterisation

\[
\sigma(K) = \frac{\alpha}{K^{1-\beta}}\left\[1 + \beta\rho\frac{\nu}{\alpha}\ln!\frac{K}{F} + \frac{1}{2}\frac{\nu^2}{\alpha^2}\left(\ln!\frac{K}{F}\right)^2\right]
]

Purpose: Empirical fit to smile data.

3.4 Term Structure of Volatility

\[
\sigma(\tau) = \text{interpolated curve (e.g., cubic spline) from market vols}
]

Purpose: Volatility as a function of timeΟΔβ²§βÄ‰toΟΔβ²§βÄ‰expiry.

3.5 GARCH(1,1) Conceptual

\[
\sigma\_t^2 = \omega + \alpha r\_{t-1}^2 + \beta \sigma\_{t-1}^2
]

Purpose: Stochastic volatility modelling for riskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰neutral simulations.

4 Greeks (Sensitivities)

4.1 FirstΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Order Greeks

| Greek | Call Formula | Put Formula |
|-------|--------------|-------------|
| Delta (\Delta = \frac{\partial V}{\partial S}) | (e^{-qT}N(d\_1)) | (-e^{-qT}N(-d\_1)) |
| Gamma (\Gamma = \frac{\partial^2 V}{\partial S^2}) | (\frac{e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)}{S\sigma\sqrt{T}}) | same as call |
| Theta (\Theta = \frac{\partial V}{\partial t}) | (-\frac{S\sigma e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)}{2\sqrt{T}} - rK e^{-rT}N(d\_2) + qS e^{-qT}N(d\_1)) | (-\frac{S\sigma e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)}{2\sqrt{T}} + rK e^{-rT}N(-d\_2) - qS e^{-qT}N(-d\_1)) |
| Vega (\mathcal{V} = \frac{\partial V}{\partial \sigma}) | (S\sqrt{T},e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)) | same as call |
| Rho (\rho = \frac{\partial V}{\partial r}) | (K T e^{-rT} N(d\_2)) | (-K T e^{-rT} N(-d\_2)) |

Purpose: Core price sensitivities.

4.2 HigherΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Order & Cross Greeks

| Greek | Formula (call) |
|-------|----------------|
| Charm (\frac{\partial \Delta}{\partial t}) | ( -q e^{-qT}N(d\_1) + \frac{S\sigma e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)}{2T\sqrt{T}} \big(2q - r) ) |
| Vanna (\frac{\partial \Delta}{\partial \sigma}) | ( -\frac{e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)}{\sigma} d\_2 ) |
| Vomma (\frac{\partial \mathcal{V}}{\partial \sigma}) | ( S\sqrt{T}e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)\left(d\_1d\_2 - 1\right) ) |
| Speed (\frac{\partial^3 V}{\partial S^3}) | ( -\frac{e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)}{S^2\sigma\sqrt{T}}\left(\frac{d\_1}{\sigma\sqrt{T}} + 1\right) ) |
| Color (\frac{\partial \Gamma}{\partial t}) | ( -\frac{e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)}{2S\sigma T\sqrt{T}}\left\[\frac{2q - r}{\sigma\sqrt{T}}d\_1 - \frac{1}{\sigma\sqrt{T}} + d\_1^2 + 1\right] ) |
| Ultima (\frac{\partial^3 V}{\partial \sigma^3}) | ( S\sqrt{T}e^{-qT}\phi(d\_1)\left\[ -d\_1d\_2(d\_1^2 - d\_2^2) + d\_1^2 + d\_2^2 - 1 \right] ) |

Purpose: HigherΟΔβ²§βÄ‰order risk decomposition.

4.3 CrossΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Greek Relationships

\[
\frac{\partial \Delta}{\partial \sigma}= \text{Vanna},\qquad
\frac{\partial \mathcal{V}}{\partial S}= \text{Vanna},\qquad
\frac{\partial^2 \mathcal{V}}{\partial \sigma^2}= \text{Vomma}
]

Purpose: Consistency checks for model implementation.

4.4 Portfolio Greek Aggregation

\[
\Gamma\_{\text{port}} = \sum\_i w\_i,\Gamma\_i,\quad
\Delta\_{\text{port}} = \sum\_i w\_i,\Delta\_i,\quad
\text{etc.}
]

Purpose: Compute net sensitivity of a multiΟΔβ²§βÄ‰leg position.

5 Interest Rates & Discounting

5.1 Continuous Compounding

\[
P(0,T) = e^{-rT}
]

5.2 Discrete Compounding (m periods)

\[
P(0,T) = \left(1 + \frac{r}{m}\right)^{-mT}
]

5.3 RiskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Free Rate Assumption

(r) is the continuouslyΟΔβ²§βÄ‰compounded rate of a collateralised cash instrument with same currency as the option.

5.4 DividendΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Adjusted Pricing

Effective underlying for a dividendΟΔβ²§βÄ‰paying stock:

\[
S^{\ast}=S\_0e^{-qT}
]

5.5 CostΟΔβ²§βÄ‰ofΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Carry Model

\[
F\_T = S\_0 e^{(b-r)T},\qquad b = r - q + c
]

where (c) is any additional carry cost/benefit.

6 Probability & Distribution Functions

6.1 Standard Normal PDF

\[
\phi(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}}e^{-x^{2}/2}
]

6.2 Standard Normal CDF

\[
\Phi(x) = \int\_{-\infty}^{x}\phi(t),dt
]

6.3 Lognormal Price Distribution

\[
S\_T = S\_0\exp\Big\[(r-q-\tfrac12\sigma^2)T + \sigma\sqrt{T},Z\Big],\quad Z\sim N(0,1)
]

6.4 RiskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Neutral Probability Density

\[
f\_{S\_T}(K) = e^{-rT}\frac{1}{K}\phi!\left(\frac{\ln(S\_0/K) + (r-q)T}{\sigma\sqrt{T}}\right)
]

6.5 Expected Value under (\mathbb{Q})

\[
\mathbb{E}^{\mathbb{Q}}\[S\_T] = S\_0 e^{(r-q)T}
]

7 Exercise & Style Variations

7.1 European vs American Constraints

European: payoff only at (T).
American: payoff (\max(S\_t-K,0)) can be exercised at any (t\le T).

7.2 EarlyΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Exercise Boundary (American call with discrete dividends)

Critical price (S\_{\text{crit}}(t)) solves

\[
C\_{\text{Eur}}(S\_{\text{crit}},t) = S\_{\text{crit}} - K
]

7.3 Dividend Impact on Early Exercise

For a cash dividend (D) paid at (t\_d):

\[
S\_{\text{ex}} = K - D,e^{-r(T-t\_d)}
]

7.4 Barrier Option ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ UpΟΔβ²§βÄ‰andΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Out Call

\[
C\_{\text{UO}} = C\_{\text{BS}} - \frac{B}{S\_0}\left(\frac{S\_0}{B}\right)^{2\lambda}
e^{-\frac{(r-q+\frac12\sigma^2)^2}{\sigma^2}}N(d\_1^{\ast})
]

with (\lambda = \frac{r-q+\frac12\sigma^2}{\sigma^2}) and appropriate (d\_1^{\ast}).

7.5 Asian Option (Arithmetic Average)

Price approximated by

\[
C\_{\text{Asian}} \approx S\_0 e^{-qT} N(d\_1^{\prime}) - K e^{-rT} N(d\_2^{\prime})
]

where (d\_{1,2}^{\prime}) use adjusted volatility (\sigma\_{\text{eff}}).

7.6 Binary (Digital) Call

\[
\text{Payoff}= \begin{cases}
1 & S\_T > K\\
0 & \text{otherwise}
\end{cases}
]

ClosedΟΔβ²§βÄ‰form price:

\[
C\_{\text{binary}} = e^{-rT}\Phi!\left(\frac{\ln(S\_0/K) + (r-q)T}{\sigma\sqrt{T}}\right)
]

8 StrategyΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Level Math

8.1 BreakΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Even Points

| Strategy | BreakΟΔβ²§βÄ‰even (S\_{\text{BE}}) |
|----------|------------------------------|
| Long call | (K + \text{premium}) |
| Long put | (K - \text{premium}) |
| Bull call spread ((K\_1\<K\_2)) | (K\_1 + \text{net premium}) |

8.2 Max Profit / Max Loss

| Strategy | Max Profit | Max Loss |
|----------|------------|----------|
| Long call | (\infty) | (\text{premium}) |
| Short call | (\infty) (unlimited) | (\text{premium}) |
| Bull call spread | (K\_2-K\_1-\text{net premium}) | (\text{net premium}) |

8.3 Spread Payoff Equations

Bull Call Spread ((K\_1\<K\_2))

\[
\text{Payoff}= \max(S\_T-K\_1,0) - \max(S\_T-K\_2,0)
]

Bear Put Spread

\[
\text{Payoff}= \max(K\_1-S\_T,0) - \max(K\_2-S\_T,0)
]

8.4 Straddle / Strangle

Straddle (same strike)

\[
\text{Payoff}=|,S\_T-K,|
]

\*\*Strangle ((K\_1\<K\_2))

\[
\text{Payoff}= \begin{cases}
K\_1 - S\_T & S\_T \le K\_1\\
0 & K\_1 < S\_T < K\_2\\
S\_T - K\_2 & S\_T \ge K\_2
\end{cases}
]

8.5 Butterfly (calls)

\[
\text{Payoff}= \max(S\_T-K\_1,0) - 2\max(S\_T-K\_2,0) + \max(S\_T-K\_3,0),; K\_1\<K\_2\<K\_3
]

8.6 Condor (calls)

\[
\text{Payoff}= \max(S\_T-K\_1,0) - 2\max(S\_T-K\_2,0) + 2\max(S\_T-K\_3,0) - \max(S\_T-K\_4,0)
]

8.7 Synthetic Positions

Synthetic long stock

\[
\text{Long Call} + \text{Short Put (same }K\text{)} = S\_0 e^{-qT}
]

Synthetic forward

\[
\text{Long Call} - \text{Short Put (same }K\text{)} = e^{-rT}F\_T
]

9 PnL & Risk Metrics

9.1 MarkΟΔβ²§βÄ‰toΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Market Valuation

\[
\text{MtM}t = V(S\_t,t; \sigma{\text{imp}}, r, q)
]

9.2 SensitivityΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Based PnL Approximation

\[
\Delta \text{PnL} \approx \Delta S,\Delta + \frac12\Delta S^2,\Gamma + \Delta\sigma,\mathcal{V} + \Delta t,\Theta
]

9.3 Scenario Analysis

ReΟΔβ²§βÄ‰price the portfolio under a set of shock vectors ((\Delta S,\Delta\sigma,\Delta r,\Delta q)).

9.4 ValueΟΔβ²§βÄ‰atΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Risk (VaR) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ DeltaΟΔβ²§βÄ‰Normal

\[
\text{VaR}{\alpha} = z{\alpha},\sigma\_{\text{PnL}},\qquad
\sigma\_{\text{PnL}} = \sqrt{\Delta^2\sigma\_S^2 + \mathcal{V}^2\sigma\_{\sigma}^2 + \rho\_{\Delta\mathcal{V}}\Delta\mathcal{V}\sigma\_S\sigma\_{\sigma}}
]

9.5 Expected Shortfall (ES)

\[
\text{ES}{\alpha}= \frac{1}{1-\alpha}\int{\alpha}^{1} \text{VaR}\_u,du
]

10 Normalization & Units

10.1 Variable Definitions

| Symbol | Meaning | Typical Unit |
|--------|---------|--------------|
| (S) | Underlying spot price | currency |
| (S\_0) | Spot at time 0 | currency |
| (K) | Strike price | currency |
| (T) | Time to expiry (years, actual/365) | year |
| (t) | Current time | year |
| (r) | RiskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰free continuous rate | 1/yr |
| (q) | Continuous dividend yield | 1/yr |
| (\sigma) | Volatility (logΟΔβ²§βÄ‰normal) | 1/yr |
| (\sigma\_{\text{imp}}) | Implied volatility | 1/yr |
| (\phi(\cdot)) | Standard normal PDF | ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ |
| (\Phi(\cdot)) | Standard normal CDF | ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ |
| (N(\cdot)) | Same as (\Phi) | ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ |
| (u,d) | Binomial up/down factors | ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ |
| (p) | RiskΟΔβ²§βÄ‰neutral probability | ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ |
| (\Delta) | Delta | ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ |
| (\Gamma) | Gamma | 1/currency |
| (\Theta) | Theta | currency/yr |
| (\mathcal{V}) | Vega | currencyΟ²¬Ζyr |
| (\rho) | Rho | currencyΟ²¬Ζyr |
| (C,P) | Call/Put price | currency |
| (F\_T) | Forward price | currency |
| (P(0,T)) | Discount factor | ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ |

10.2 Core Assumptions per Formula

| Formula | Key Assumptions |
|---------|-----------------|
| BSM | Constant (r,q,\sigma); Euclidean geometry; no early exercise |
| Binomial | Lognormal price dynamics approximated by discrete steps; no arbitrage |
| Monte Carlo | Sufficient number of paths for convergence; independent draws |
| Greeks | Differentiability of price w.r.t. underlying parameters |
| Historical vol | Returns i.i.d.; annualisation factor 252 trading days |
| Implied vol | Market is arbitrageΟΔβ²§βÄ‰free; BSM model holds for extraction |
| SABR | Volatility smile is smooth; parameters (\alpha,\beta,\rho,\nu) constant across strikes |
| GARCH | MeanΟΔβ²§βÄ‰reverting volatility; constant parameters |

End of consolidation.
